source: College Scorecard (U.S. Department of Education) + Modeled Scholarship Scenarios
description: Institution-level scholarship optimization analysis showing optimal discount levels for revenue maximization and projected enrollment/revenue impacts across discount scenarios.

unit_id.description: Unique institution identifier from College Scorecard

pell_share.description: Share of undergraduates receiving Pell Grants (federal aid for low-income students)

retention_rate.description: Share of first-time, full-time students returning for second year

cost_of_attendance.description: Average annual total cost (tuition, fees, room, board, books, supplies)

sticker_tuition_proxy.description: Proxy for sticker tuition (list price before aid); used as basis for revenue calculations

baseline_incoming_class.description: Baseline entering cohort size (students expected to enroll without scholarship intervention)

baseline_yield.description: Baseline yield rate (percentage of admitted students who enroll)

price_sensitivity.description: Elasticity of yield to price (scholarship discount). Higher values indicate greater sensitivity to price reductions. Estimated from institutional characteristics and historical patterns.

interpretation.high: >1.25: High price sensitivity (less selective, price-dependent recruitment)

optimal_discount_pct.description: Scholarship discount level (as percentage of sticker tuition) that maximizes net tuition revenue for this institution

optimal_projected_yield.description: Projected yield rate when optimal discount is applied

optimal_projected_enrollment.description: Projected entering class size when optimal discount is applied

optimal_net_tuition.description: Net tuition per student after optimal discount (sticker_tuition_proxy x (1 - optimal_discount_pct))

optimal_net_revenue.description: Total net tuition revenue with optimal discount (optimal_projected_enrollment x optimal_net_tuition)

optimal_incremental_enrollment.description: Additional students attracted by optimal discount (optimal_projected_enrollment - baseline_incoming_class)

optimal_revenue_change_pct.description: Percentage change in net revenue from applying optimal discount vs. baseline (no discount). Positive values indicate revenue improvement; negative indicate revenue decline.

interpretation.positive: Scholarship strategy increases revenue (enrollment gain exceeds revenue loss)
interpretation.near_zero: Scholarship strategy has minimal revenue impact
interpretation.negative: Scholarship strategy decreases revenue (enrollment gain insufficient to offset per-student loss)

optimal_discount_pct.calculation: Discount level (0%-100%) that maximizes: optimal_projected_enrollment x optimal_net_tuition
optimal_discount_pct.methodology: Revenue optimization model iterates through discount scenarios and selects level producing highest net revenue

optimal_projected_yield.interpretation: Yield cannot exceed 100% (biological maximum); larger discounts produce proportionally larger yield improvements for price-sensitive institutions

optimal_revenue_change_pct.note: Positive: Discounts increase revenue (counter-intuitive for revenue-conscious institutions). Negative: Discounts reduce revenue (common for selective institutions). Zero: Discounts have minimal net impact.

data_quality_notes: Price sensitivity coefficients are modeled estimates based on institutional characteristics and market patterns; actual price sensitivity may vary
Revenue projections assume linear enrollment response to discounts; actual response may be non-linear
Model focuses on net tuition revenue and doesn't account for room & board, fees, retention impacts, or cost of instruction
Institutional-level aggregates mask variation by degree program; optimal discount for engineering may differ from optimal for liberal arts
Model assumes independent institutional decisions; actual competitive dynamics may make calculated optimal discounts sub-optimal in equilibrium
strategic_use_cases: Revenue optimization: Determine scholarship discount level that maximizes net tuition revenue for institution type and market position
Competitive benchmarking: Compare your institution's current strategy to optimal strategy and to peer institutions' optimal strategies
Enrollment planning: Model enrollment and revenue impacts of alternative scholarship discount levels
Demographic strategy: Evaluate how scholarships can attract targeted populations (e.g., first-generation, specific geographic regions)
Financial forecasting: Scenario modeling of revenue impacts under different price/enrollment strategies
limitations: This model prioritizes revenue maximization; other institutional goals (access, equity, mission) may warrant different strategies
Assumes no competitive response; if all institutions increase discounts simultaneously, projected enrollment gains may not materialize
Long-term impacts not captured: student completion, retention, alumni outcomes, reputation effects
Doesn't distinguish merit-based scholarships (talent recruitment) from need-based scholarships (access) or other types
Regional and degree-program variation masked in institution-level aggregates
Model recency: Based on College Scorecard recent cohorts; market conditions may have changed
